column221:
I’m most at peace when I’m near a large body of water. I was born near the Gulf of Mexico, raised on Chesapeake Bay, learned to swim in the lakes of Wisconsin, lived on the lake in Washington, on the river in Oregon, on the Bay in Northern CA, and always within 3 miles of the shore in Southern CA.
We make the sacrifice of a larger house and yard to live close. Our well being as a whole is affected by the waters. Even though our air is cleaner,  it seems the beaches and water are increasingly polluted compared to when I first moved to CA in the 90s. 
The number of unhoused that find refuge at the beach, drug use, alcohol - makes it unsafe to use the facilities or visit at times.
I fully believe the shift we saw in marine wildlife during COVID is indicative as to the harm human activity has on the water.

column221: I am a passionate freediver, kayaker, and spear fisherman. I use the ocean for recreation as well as sustenance. I believe being in the ocean is the equivalent to going to church, it is a religion and a lifestyle. I come from a working class immigrant family, and I love that there are many opportunities for people of the same background to access the ocean and its bounty. However, I wish there were more areas that could be accessed for fishing for those without the money/means for a vessel/boat. There are too many MPAs is my opinion that limit the access for those that want to fish but don’t have a boat. I wish that was not the case because it feels like accessing the coast for fishing is a rich man’s hobby. Only those with vessels worth tens of thousands of dollars are able to access the productive fishing areas. Most of the open take areas that are accessible from shore are sparse with life and it’s very competitive. So I hope to see some changes in the future.

column221: I studied environmental analysis and have done work for NOAA and CINMS. I strongly believe that we need to carefully and collectively manage marine species, resources, and coastal resilience and wellbeing. I am extremely concerned about federal cuts to funding for marine and coastal resilience and see this as an existential threat to our collective futures. I am also concerned about water quality as a surfer and how runoff especially after rain impacts water quality. I think there needs to be more public awareness on and before getting to the beach on safety of water quality and rip currents, etc. Displacement due to rising costs is horrendous and so are decisions to protect the environment that don’t take human dimensions into consideration. Thank you for the work that you are doing.

column221: I grew up in North Long Beach around the 710 and 91 freeway. My family and I had asthma growing up and the ocean was one of the ways I felt relief from this pollution and one of the earliest ways I felt a connection to nature. Fast forward to now I work in the environmental field, I’m a plein air painter and make art about conservation, and I photograph wildlife especially birds. The ocean and the protection of its inhabitants, both people and wildlife, is so important to me.
